description_long: This object is a rectangular, flat sheet with ten evenly spaced, raised, dome-shaped blisters arranged in two rows of five. The blisters are set into a thin, rigid backing, likely made of plastic or foil, and are designed to individually encase and protect solid medicine doses such as tablets or capsules. The surface is smooth and slightly reflective, with each blister forming a distinct compartment. The backing appears to be a single piece with no visible perforations or openings, suggesting it is an intact, unopened package. There is no visible text, branding, or color variation on the surface, indicating a generic or unbranded appearance. The overall form is compact and lightweight, suitable for easy handling and transport in urban environments.
description: A rectangular, flat blister pack with ten raised, dome-shaped compartments in two rows, made of smooth, reflective material.
description_view_0: Front view shows the ten evenly spaced, dome-shaped blisters in two rows, with the flat backing visible around the edges.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the height and curvature of the blisters, showing their rounded, protruding shape above the backing.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the arrangement of the blisters and the flat backing, with no visible openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again emphasizes the domed blisters' height and smooth, reflective surface.
